flashcards.front: What is a partial order relation?
flashcards.back: A relation R on a set A that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive. It establishes a hierarchical ordering among elements where some elements may be incomparable.
A pair (A, R) where A is a set and R is a partial order relation on A. The set is equipped with an ordering that may not relate all pairs of elements.
A graphical representation of a finite poset where elements are vertices, and covering relations are edges. It simplifies the digraph by omitting transitive edges and arrowheads.

flashcards.front: Define a maximal element in a poset.
flashcards.back: An element m in poset (A, ≤) is maximal if there is no element a in A such that m < a. No other element is strictly greater than m.

flashcards.front: Define a minimal element in a poset.
flashcards.back: An element m in poset (A, ≤) is minimal if there is no element a in A such that a < m. No other element is strictly less than m.

flashcards.front: What is the greatest element in a poset?
flashcards.back: An element g in poset (A, ≤) such that for all a in A, a ≤ g. It is unique if it exists and is comparable with every element.

flashcards.front: What is the least element in a poset?
flashcards.back: An element l in poset (A, ≤) such that for all a in A, l ≤ a. It is unique if it exists and is comparable with every element.

flashcards.front: Define an upper bound of a subset S in a poset.
flashcards.back: An element u in poset (A, ≤) is an upper bound of S ⊆ A if for all s in S, s ≤ u. It need not belong to S.

flashcards.front: Define a lower bound of a subset S in a poset.
flashcards.back: An element l in poset (A, ≤) is a lower bound of S ⊆ A if for all s in S, l ≤ s. It need not belong to S.
A poset where every pair of elements has a least upper bound (join) and greatest lower bound (meet). Every finite nonempty lattice has both a least and greatest element.

flashcards.front: Define a chain in a poset.
flashcards.back: A subset C of a poset where every pair of elements is comparable. In a chain, for any two elements a, b, either a ≤ b or b ≤ a.

flashcards.front: Define an antichain in a poset.
flashcards.back: A subset A of a poset where no two distinct elements are comparable. All elements in an antichain are pairwise incomparable.

flashcards.front: What is the join of two elements in a lattice?
flashcards.back: The least upper bound (supremum) of two elements a and b, denoted a ∨ b. It is the smallest element that is greater than or equal to both a and b.

flashcards.front: What is the meet of two elements in a lattice?
flashcards.back: The greatest lower bound (infimum) of two elements a and b, denoted a ∧ b. It is the largest element that is less than or equal to both a and b.

flashcards.front: What is a covering relation in a poset?
flashcards.back: Element x covers y if y < x and there is no element z such that y < z < x. In Hasse diagrams, edges represent covering relations directly.
